methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
ADASI, a sovereign-funded defense and security company at the EDGE stage, operates within a sector comprising 24 peers total: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities. The private peer set includes Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries, though these represent a heterogeneous group spanning different sub-verticals and development stages.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to ADASI. The analysis identified zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers meeting the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for reliable median estimation. While 24 same-sector peers exist broadly in Defense & Security, the narrow filtering criteria—necessary to ensure methodological rigor—eliminated all candidates. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given ADASI's specialized sovereign-funding status and EDGE-stage positioning.

No triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from the available data. The core limitation is the absence of a sufficiently tight peer cohort with publicly disclosed valuation metrics. To support a credible analysis, additional comparable data would be required either from recent ADASI transaction activity, similar-stage sovereign-backed defense programs, or expanded disclosure from the existing private peer set. Until such data materializes, valuation assessment must remain deferred.
